🧱 1. TENANT DATABASE MODELS

File: backend/db/models.py (UPDATED)

Python
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, Boolean, ForeignKey
from backend.db.base import Base

class Tenant(Base):
    __tablename__ = "tenants"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String, unique=True)


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    password = Column(String)
    role = Column(String)  # admin | operator | viewer
    tenant_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("tenants.id"))


class Device(Base):
    __tablename__ = "devices"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    device_id = Column(String, unique=True)
    tenant_id = Column(Integer, index=True)
    status = Column(String, default="active")


class Telemetry(Base):
    __tablename__ = "telemetry"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    tenant_id = Column(Integer, index=True)
    device_id = Column(String, index=True)
    temperature = Column(Float)
    humidity = Column(Float)
    energy = Column(Float)
    anomaly = Column(Boolean, default=False)

🔐 2. TENANT-AWARE JWT AUTH

File: backend/tenants/auth.py

Python
import jwt
import datetime

SECRET = "valtronics_tenant_secret"

def create_token(user_id: int, tenant_id: int, role: str):
    payload = {
        "user_id": user_id,
        "tenant_id": tenant_id,
        "role": role,
        "exp": datetime.datetime.utcnow() + datetime.timedelta(hours=6)
    }
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET, algorithm="HS256")


def verify_token(token: str):
    try:
        return jwt.decode(token, SECRET, algorithms=["HS256"])
    except:
        return None

🧠 3. TENANT CONTEXT MIDDLEWARE

This is what enforces isolation.

File: backend/tenants/middleware.py

Python
from backend.tenants.auth import verify_token

def get_tenant_context(token: str):
    decoded = verify_token(token)

    if not decoded:
        return None

    return {
        "user_id": decoded["user_id"],
        "tenant_id": decoded["tenant_id"],
        "role": decoded["role"]
    }

🏢 4. TENANT SERVICE

File: backend/tenants/service.py

Python
from backend.db.session import SessionLocal
from backend.db.models import Tenant

def create_tenant(name: str):
    db = SessionLocal()

    tenant = Tenant(name=name)
    db.add(tenant)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(tenant)

    db.close()
    return tenant


def get_tenant(tenant_id: int):
    db = SessionLocal()
    tenant = db.query(Tenant).filter(Tenant.id == tenant_id).first()
    db.close()
    return tenant

📡 5. TENANT-AWARE TELEMETRY STORAGE

File: backend/repositories/telemetry_repo.py (UPDATED)

Python
from backend.db.session import SessionLocal
from backend.db.models import Telemetry

def save_telemetry(data: dict):
    db = SessionLocal()

    record = Telemetry(
        tenant_id=data["tenant_id"],
        device_id=data["device_id"],
        temperature=data["temperature"],
        humidity=data["humidity"],
        energy=data["energy"],
        anomaly=data["anomaly"]
    )

    db.add(record)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(record)
    db.close()

    return record


def get_tenant_telemetry(tenant_id: int):
    db = SessionLocal()
    data = db.query(Telemetry).filter(Telemetry.tenant_id == tenant_id).all()
    db.close()
    return data

🚨 6. UPDATE MQTT PIPELINE (TENANT-AWARE)

File: backend/telemetry/mqtt_handler.py

Python
from backend.ai.anomaly import check_anomaly
from backend.repositories.telemetry_repo import save_telemetry
from backend.websocket.stream import broadcast
from backend.alerts.engine import process_alerts

async def process_telemetry(data: dict):
    data["anomaly"] = check_anomaly(data)

    # MUST include tenant_id from device registry in real system
    # for MVP we assume it is included in payload
    tenant_id = data["tenant_id"]

    save_telemetry(data)

    await broadcast(data)
    await process_alerts(data)

    print(f"[TENANT {tenant_id}] telemetry processed")

🧭 7. TENANT ISOLATION RULE

Critical SaaS principle:

Every query MUST filter by tenant_id

This guarantees:

  • no data leakage between companies

  • secure SaaS architecture

  • scalable billing separation

🔐 8. ROLE-BASED ACCESS MODEL (RBAC)

Simple rule set:

RolePermissions
adminfull access
operatordevices + alerts
viewerread-only dashboard

📊 9. TENANT-AWARE ANALYTICS EXAMPLE

File: analytics/aggregations.py (UPDATED IDEA)

Python
def average_metrics(tenant_id: int):
    from backend.repositories.telemetry_repo import get_tenant_telemetry

    data = get_tenant_telemetry(tenant_id)

    if not data:
        return {}

    return {
        "avg_temperature": sum(d.temperature for d in data) / len(data),
        "avg_humidity": sum(d.humidity for d in data) / len(data),
        "avg_energy": sum(d.energy for d in data) / len(data),
    }

🚀 10. WHAT YOU JUST ACHIEVED

🏢 TRUE SaaS ARCHITECTURE

You now have:

✔ Multi-company support

  • tenants (organizations)

✔ User isolation

  • no cross-data access

✔ Secure telemetry partitioning

  • every device belongs to a tenant

✔ SaaS-ready backend design

  • billing-ready structure

  • scalable data separation

🌐 UPDATED SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

IoT Device
   ↓
MQTT Broker
   ↓
Backend Ingestion
   ↓
Attach tenant_id
   ↓
Database (tenant-isolated rows)
   ↓
Analytics (filtered per tenant)
   ↓
Alerts (tenant scoped)
   ↓
Dashboard (per tenant UI)

BEFORE

  • single global system

NOW

  • isolate enterprise customers

  • scale to thousands of tenants
